ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic pizza class (FOODON_00002572). No specific subclass for thin-crust meat pizza exists; broader class used as the closest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers pizza as a category. No specific thin-crust meat variant code exists; broader category code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for thin-crust meat pizza; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

main_ingredients: processed meat toppings (e.g. pepperoni, salami, ham)

serving.description: 1 slice (approximately 1/8 of a standard 30 cm pizza)
serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving is one slice of a standard 30 cm thin-crust meat pizza, weighing approximately 80 g.

nutrient_source.title: Thin Crust Pizza (Meat) — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id F05020113 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. All per_100g core macronutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre) are taken directly from the HPB record. Saturated fat (6.5 g/100 g) and trans fat (0.1 g/100 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy reference entry for meat pizza (NDB 21299) as HPB does not separately report these fatty acid fractions for this item. All other micronutrient and fatty acid fields not reported by HPB Foodo for this item are set to -1 and documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 — pizza, thin crust, cheese (analogous estimate for thin-crust meat pizza)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database reports GI = 36 (low) for thin-crust cheese pizza (Atkinson et al., Am J Clin Nutr 2008; Foster-Powell et al., AJCN 2002). Thin-crust meat pizza has a comparable crust and carbohydrate matrix; the addition of processed meat toppings does not materially alter the GI relative to the cheese-pizza reference. GL per serving calculated as: GI (36) × carbohydrate per serving (25.81 g/100 g × 80 g serving = 20.6 g) / 100 = 7.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 718 mg per 100 g — well above the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-sensitive foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit thin-crust meat pizza due to its very high sodium content (718 mg/100 g), driven by processed meat toppings, mozzarella cheese, and tomato sauce; if consumed, restrict to one small slice and avoid all other high-sodium foods in the same meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined-flour thin crust with 25.8 g carbohydrate per 100 g and only 0.9 g dietary fibre, yielding a low-GI (36) but moderate glycaemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should limit portion size to one slice (approximately 80 g), pair with a high-fibre salad to blunt the postprandial glucose response, and note that the high saturated fat from cheese and processed meat may worsen insulin resistance with regular consumption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~6.5 g/100 g), estimated trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g from processed meats), and very high sodium (718 mg/100 g) collectively elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should minimise consumption of thin-crust meat pizza due to its combination of high saturated fat, trace trans fat from processed meats, and very high sodium, all of which adversely affect LDL cholesterol, arterial stiffness, and blood pressure.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pro-inflammatory profile: saturated fat ~6.5 g/100 g, trans fat ~0.1 g/100 g, arachidonic acid from processed meats, and high sodium 718 mg/100 g
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid processed meat toppings (pepperoni, salami) due to their high arachidonic acid and trans fat content, both of which promote systemic inflammation and may worsen joint symptoms; substituting with vegetables or plain grilled chicken significantly reduces the inflammatory load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Thin crust pizza with meat toppings is a baked Western-style pizza characterised by a thin, crisp refined-wheat-flour base topped with tomato sauce, mozzarella cheese, and processed meat toppings such as pepperoni, salami, or ham. The thin crust produces a crunchy texture with a slightly chewy interior, and the combination of melted cheese and savoury cured meats delivers a rich, salty, umami-forward flavour profile. Per 100 g, it provides 310 kcal, 15.8 g protein, 15.9 g fat (of which approximately 6.5 g is saturated), 25.8 g carbohydrate, 0.9 g dietary fibre, and 718 mg sodium. The GI is approximately 36 (low), but the high sodium content (718 mg/100 g) makes it clinically unsuitable for individuals with hypertension. The saturated fat and trans fat from processed meats raise cardiovascular and rheumatoid arthritis concerns. For people with type 2 diabetes, the low GI is partially offset by the low fibre content and the insulin-resistance risk from chronic high saturated fat intake. In Singapore and globally, thin-crust meat pizza is widely available as a takeaway or delivery meal and is commonly consumed as a main course at lunch or dinner. A standard serving is approximately one slice (80 g), contributing roughly 248 kcal and 574 mg sodium per slice.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre) are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database with a reviewed status and confidence of 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from the closest USDA SR Legacy reference for meat pizza and documented accordingly. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for thin-crust cheese pizza, which is the closest approved GI reference available.
notes: G3 COMPLIANCE — ALL -1 FIELDS DOCUMENTED: The following per_100g fields are set to -1 because the data is not available in the primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, cr_id F05020113) for this specific food item, and the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy reference (NDB 21299, meat pizza) does not provide a sufficiently close match for all micronutrients to justify populating them with adapted values: monounsaturated_fat_g (-1): detailed fatty acid fractionation not reported by HPB Foodo for this item; not separately available in the HPB record. polyunsaturated_fat_g (-1): same reason as monounsaturated_fat_g; HPB Foodo does not report PUFA breakdown for this item. potassium_mg (-1): not reported in HPB Foodo for this item; no approved-source value available for direct attribution. calcium_mg (-1): not reported in HPB Foodo for this item. iron_mg (-1): not reported in HPB Foodo for this item. cholesterol_mg (-1): not reported in HPB Foodo for this item. vitamin_c_mg (-1): not reported in HPB Foodo for this item. vitamin_a_mcg_rae (-1): not reported in HPB Foodo for this item. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg (-1): not reported in HPB Foodo for this item. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg (-1): not reported in HPB Foodo for this item. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg (-1): not reported in HPB Foodo for this item. vitamin_b6_mg (-1): not reported in HPB Foodo for this item. vitamin_b12_mcg (-1): not reported in HPB Foodo for this item. folate_mcg_dfe (-1): not reported in HPB Foodo for this item. vitamin_d_mcg (-1): not reported in HPB Foodo for this item. vitamin_e_mg (-1): not reported in HPB Foodo for this item. phosphorus_mg (-1): not reported in HPB Foodo for this item. magnesium_mg (-1): not reported in HPB Foodo for this item. zinc_mg (-1): not reported in HPB Foodo for this item. selenium_mcg (-1): not reported in HPB Foodo for this item. water_g (-1): moisture content not reported in HPB Foodo for this item. Source consulted and confirming unavailability: HPB Singapore Foodo database (https://foodo.hpb.gov.sg/), accessed 2026-04-16. ADDITIONAL NOTES: (1) Saturated fat (6.5 g/100 g) and trans fat (0.1 g/100 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for meat pizza (NDB 21299), as the HPB Foodo record does not separately report these fatty acid fractions. These values are consistent with the input record's health_suitability reasoning. (2) This is a Western cuisine food; multilingual local-name requirements (Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, Vietnamese) do not apply. All local_names fields are correctly set to null. (3) GI of 36 is an analogous estimate based on thin-crust cheese pizza from the University of Sydney GI Database / Foster-Powell et al. (AJCN 2002); no direct GI measurement for thin-crust meat pizza was found in the approved GI source. (4) Halal status is marked false as processed meat toppings (pepperoni, salami) typically contain pork; halal-certified variants exist but are not the default assumption for a generic record.
